vulnerability.reason: Both describe unsupervised reliance on NR-IQA without user verification, leaving manipulated scores undetected.
vulnerability.reference: Photo apps use NR-IQA scores to rank images without user override or cross-check.
vulnerability.generated: Unsupervised reliance on blind IQA for automatic album curation leaves end-users unable to detect manipulated quality scores.

impact.reason: User dissatisfaction and reduced engagement are covered, but negative reviews are omitted.
impact.reference: Poorly organized photo collections reduce user satisfaction, app engagement, and lead to negative reviews.
impact.generated: Users receive poorly curated smart albums with irrelevant top images, causing frustration, decreased satisfaction, and abandonment of the feature.

dose_response.reason: Same causal logic: perturbations corrupt quality scores, leading to misranked/poorly curated albums, causing user frustration and disengagement.
dose_response.reference: Adversarial perturbations skew NR-IQA scores, leading to shuffled image ranking in photo apps, which degrades user satisfaction, reduces engagement, and prompts negative reviews.
dose_response.generated: Adversarial perturbations in blind IQA models, through end-user exposure in photo library apps, trigger unsupervised reliance that prevents detection of manipulated scores, leading to poorly curated smart albums, frustration, and feature abandonment.
